contract.storey_confirmed: Single — confirmed in Jim's Building Inspections 137B report
contract.orientation_confirmed: North West (from 137B report) — less ideal for winter solar gain
recent_owner_builder_works: Renovation of bathroom and ensuite in January 2026
New floating timber floor, carpet, and painting throughout in January 2026
contract.owner_builder_report: No safety hazard, defect, incomplete works, or recycled building materials found in the supplied February 2026 report. Undetected defect risk: Low.
contract.owner_builder_report_caveat: Visual inspection only — inspector could not verify waterproofing method behind new bathroom/ensuite tiles. Waterproofing failures typically take 2-3 years to manifest. Independent B&P must specifically target this area.
contract_watchpoints: Single storey confirmed — this violates the preferred double-storey floor plan filter. Visit only if treating as a calibration/benchmark property.
Flip pattern: bought Sept 2025, renovated Jan 2026, sold May 2026. Ask agent directly: why selling after 8 months and 4 months post-renovation?

137B waterproofing caveat: inspector could not verify what is behind the new bathroom tiles. Commission independent B&P and specifically ask inspector to assess moisture/waterproofing risk in and around the new wet areas.
Older 1987 shell: brick veneer, Colorbond roof — check hot water system age, switchboard, roof condition, and asbestos risk (pre-1988 materials).
Large 653m2 land — more maintenance burden than preferred (300-550m2 target).
No planning overlays, no OC, no covenant — title is the cleanest of the three.

next_action: Inspect Saturday as a value/space benchmark; decide whether the renovated interior and strong office outweigh the older shell and large-yard maintenance mismatch.
